Little Women (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $108,095,955Details
International Box Office $110,742,431
Worldwide Box Office $218,838,386
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,324,448
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,619,616
Total $8,944,064
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The story unfolds as the author’s alter ego, Jo March, reflects back and forth on her fictional life. In this take, the beloved story of the March sisters — four young women each determined to live life on her own terms — is both timeless and timely.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$16,755,310 (15.5% of total gross)
Legs:6.45 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:49.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$42,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,308 opening theaters/3,308 max. theaters, 6.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $140,937,836

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2019 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: December 25th, 2019 (Wide) (Jamaica)
December 25th, 2019 (Wide), released as Mujercitas (Spain)
December 26th, 2019 (Wide) (Aruba)
December 26th, 2019 (Wide) (Curacao)
December 26th, 2019 (Wide) (Suriname)
Video Release: March 24th, 2020 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
May 25th, 2020 by Sony Pictures
MPA Rating: PG for thematic elements and brief smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2593 (Cert #51940), 8/28/2019)
Running Time: 135 minutes
Award nomination: 2020 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: F-Rated, F-Rated, Triple
Geographical setting: Massachusetts, New York, New York City
Plot point: Coming of Age, Death of a Sibling, Non-Chronological, Relationships Gone Wrong, Romance, Writing and Writers
Social setting: Christmas
Subgenre: Historical Drama
Time period setting: 1860s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Regency Enterprises, Pascal Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

The story unfolds as the author’s alter ego, Jo March, reflects back and forth on her fictional life. In this take, the beloved story of the March sisters — four young women each determined to live life on her own terms — is both timeless and timely.
